reforms.description: The federal government committed $600 million to the Syncrude consortium to facilitate further development of Alberta's oilsands, which is expected to enhance technological advancements in the energy sector.
reforms.growth_orientation_rationale: The investment is aimed at enhancing technological advancements, which can lead to increased productivity and innovation in the energy sector.
reforms.status_evidence: The funding has been committed and is currently facilitating development.
reforms.importance_rationale: The funding represents a significant investment in sectoral R&D but does not constitute a major structural change.
reforms.source_quote: Federal, Ontario and Alberta governments commit $600 million to the Syncrude consortium to permit further development of Alberta oilsands.

reforms.description: The Federal Housing Action Programme was announced, which included the expansion of the Assisted Home Ownership Programme to ensure eligibility for two-person households and increase the maximum annual subsidy for mortgage repayments.
reforms.growth_orientation_rationale: The expansion of the housing programme is expected to support home ownership, which can stimulate economic activity and innovation in the housing sector.
reforms.status_evidence: The programme has been announced and is currently in effect.
reforms.importance_rationale: The programme represents a major reform in housing policy aimed at increasing accessibility and support for home ownership.
reforms.source_quote: Federal Housing Action Programme announced consisting of: (i) Expansion of the Assisted Home Ownership Programme (AHOP) whereby eligibility was ensured to two-person households, and the maximum annual subsidy available to bring the subsidised 8 per cent mortgage repayments down to less than 25 per cent of the borrowers income was increased from $600 to $750.
